title: Solid-Fuel Propulsion Technology Development
entry_mechanism: GADD-led R&D programme, Phase II beginning; production capability from Phase III
executive_summary:
Solid-fuel rocket propulsion — the technology that drives ballistic missiles, tactical rockets, and solid-fuel boosters for precision-guided munitions — is one of the most strategically sensitive and technically demanding capabilities in the defence industrial ecosystem. Saudi Arabia currently has zero sovereign solid-fuel propulsion capability, making it entirely dependent on imported propulsion systems for every rocket and missile in its inventory.

Node MS-3 is a long-duration technology development programme, not an immediate manufacturing investment. The goal is to establish Saudi Arabia's first sovereign solid-fuel propulsion research and development capability by Phase II, transitioning to limited production capability by Phase III. The technology pathway begins with solid-fuel chemistry and motor design research, progresses through small motor production and testing, and culminates in production-scale capability for tactical rocket motors.

The strategic importance is clear: a nation that cannot produce its own propulsion systems cannot claim sovereign missile capability. Saudi Arabia's relationship with ballistic missile technology (including its existing CSS-5 inventory) makes this node particularly sensitive — engagement requires the highest-level government-to-government framework and NSCS oversight throughout.

companies.name: Safran Propulsion (ArianeGroup)
companies.rationale: ArianeGroup / Safran has solid-fuel propulsion technology for space and tactical applications. France's historical Saudi Arabia defence relationship supports technology transfer discussions at the government level.

companies.name: MBDA
companies.rationale: MBDA integrates solid-fuel motors into tactical missiles supplied to Saudi Arabia. MBDA has technology transfer experience in allied nations.

companies.name: Nammo (Nordic Ammunition Company)
companies.rationale: Nammo develops and manufactures solid rocket motors for European tactical systems. Norwegian technology is non-ITAR and Nammo has international partnerships.

companies.name: Bayern-Chemie / TDW
companies.rationale: Bayern-Chemie is Europe's leading solid-fuel propellant research company. A research partnership for Saudi propellant chemistry capability.

companies.name: ROXEL (French/UK)
companies.rationale: Roxel manufactures solid-fuel motors for European tactical missiles including MICA and VL Mica. Technology transfer discussions are politically feasible.

partners.name: GADD (General Authority for Defence Development)
partners.current: Defence R&D programme management; some energetic materials research through KACST
partners.upgrade: Establish GADD Propulsion Technology Centre; recruit 20 Saudi propulsion engineers (GADD fellowship in France, Norway); Phase II: solid-fuel chemistry research lab; Phase III: small motor production
partners.timeline: Phase II: 36 months to research capability; Phase III: 60+ months to production

partners.name: KACST / Advanced Materials Research
partners.current: Energetic materials research; polymer chemistry; some combustion research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST propellant chemistry programme; solid-fuel formulation research; combustion instability research; propulsion test facility

partners.name: SABIC Research and Innovation
partners.current: Polymer chemistry; petrochemical research; materials science
partners.upgrade: GADD-SABIC energetic materials precursor research; Saudi-produced propellant binder materials; oxidiser supply chain development

partners.name: KFUPM Chemical Engineering
partners.current: Chemical engineering; combustion research; some energetic materials courses
partners.upgrade: GADD-KFUPM propulsion engineering programme; 15 PhD students in propulsion chemistry; first Saudi propulsion engineers pipeline
